title: Ensure that the --insecure-bind-address argument is not set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --insecure-bind-address argument is not set
input_row.description: Do not bind to insecure addresses.
input_row.rationale: If you bind the federation apiserver to an insecure address, basically anyone who could connect to it over the insecure port, would have unauthenticated and unencrypted access to it. The federation apiserver doesn't do any authentication checking for insecure binds and neither the insecure traffic is encrypted. Hence, you should not bind the federation apiserver to an insecure address.
input_row.audit: Run the following command: ps -ef | grep federation-apiserver Verify that the --insecure-bind-address argument does not exist or is set to 127.0.0.1.
input_row.remediation: Edit the deployment specs and remove --insecure-bind-address. kubectl edit deployments federation-apiserver-deployment -- namespace=federation-system Impact: None Default Value: By default, insecure bind address is set to 127.0.0.1. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/federation-apiserver/  191 | P a g e 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/federation/manifests/fe deration-apiserver-deployment.yaml 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/
input_row.impact: None Default Value: By default, insecure bind address is set to 127.0.0.1.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_federation_apiserver_insecure_bind_address_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the federation-apiserver Deployment in the federation-system namespace using the Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- Inspect the container spec(s) for the federation-apiserver pod(s) and enumerate the command and args fields.
- Specifically, check for the presence of the --insecure-bind-address argument:
  - Pass if --insecure-bind-address is not present, or if it is set to 127.0.0.1.
  - Fail if --insecure-bind-address is set to any value other than 127.0.0.1.
- Example kubectl command:
  ```
  kubectl -n federation-system get deployment federation-apiserver-deployment -o json | jq '.spec.template.spec.containers[].args'
  ```
- Alternatively, use the Kubernetes API to retrieve the Deployment spec and parse the args/command fields for the federation-apiserver container.
- Pass/Fail Criteria:
  - Pass: --insecure-bind-address is absent or set to 127.0.0.1
  - Fail: --insecure-bind-address is set to any other address (e.g., 0.0.0.0, public IP, etc.)
